chapter_context.summary: David's grand song of praise and thanksgiving to the LORD for deliverance from all his enemies and from the hand of Saul (parallel to Psalm 18).

outline.text: David's Song of Deliverance; Praise to the Rock
outline.explanation: This section focuses on David's Song of Deliverance; Praise to the Rock (22:1-4), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Samuel.

outline.text: Praise for Deliverance from Death and Saul's Hand
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Praise for Deliverance from Death and Saul's Hand (22:5-20), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Samuel.

outline.text: Praise for God's Reward According to Cleanness of Hands
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Praise for God's Reward According to Cleanness of Hands (22:21-30), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Samuel.

outline.text: Praise for God's Strength and Military Victories
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Praise for God's Strength and Military Victories (22:31-46), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Samuel.

outline.text: Exaltation to the God of Salvation and David's Dynasty
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Exaltation to the God of Salvation and David's Dynasty (22:47-51), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Samuel.

key_themes.title: The Davidic Covenant
key_themes.description: God's unconditional promise of an eternal dynasty, ultimately pointing forward to the Messiah.

key_themes.title: Sin and Consequences
key_themes.description: While God offers complete forgiveness for sincere repentance, the earthly consequences of sin remain.

devotional.question: How do the events of Chapter 22 demonstrate the grace of God in the midst of human failure?
What lessons about leadership and accountability can you apply to your own life from this chapter?

cultural_historical.title: Jerusalem as Capital
cultural_historical.text: By capturing Jerusalem, a neutral city on the border of Judah and Benjamin, David strategically united the fractured northern and southern tribes under a single capital.
